abort.accept_shared_reauth_required: Re-authentication is required before the shared device can be accepted. Sign in again, then retry.
abort.already_configured: This Jackery account is already configured.
abort.already_in_progress: A configuration flow for this Jackery account is already in progress.
abort.reauth_entry_missing: The Jackery entry for re-authentication was not found.
abort.reconfigure_account_mismatch: The new email does not match the configured Jackery account. Use the original email or remove the entry first.
abort.reconfigure_entry_missing: The Jackery entry for reconfiguration was not found.
abort.shelly_auth_url_failed: Could not start Shelly pairing. Please try again later.
abort.shelly_no_devices: No Shelly accessories were bound. Make sure you completed the Shelly authorization, then try again.
abort.shelly_reauth_required: Re-authentication with the Jackery account is required before pairing Shelly accessories.
abort.shelly_successful: Shelly accessories paired; the integration was reloaded.

error.accept_shared_failed: Could not accept the shared device. Check the device ID and QR code ID and try again.

accept_shared.description: Accept a SolarVault that was shared with the Jackery account {username}. Enter the device ID and QR code ID from the sharing invitation. The integration reloads afterwards so the shared device's entities appear.
accept_shared.title: Accept a shared device

reauth_confirm.description: Your saved password for {username} no longer works. Enter the current password to restore the connection.
reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Jackery

data_description.create_calculated_power_sensors: Creates optional local net-power sensors from values already delivered by Jackery: main battery charge minus discharge, battery-system/stack charge minus discharge, and grid-side input minus output. These sensors are useful for dashboards that need a signed net value, but they are not separate app values. Disabled by default so raw Jackery values stay unambiguous.
data_description.create_savings_detail_sensors: Creates optional sensors for the savings calculation: calculated savings, self-consumed AC energy, price, battery system loss energy, inverter/conversion loss energy, PV residual after self-consumption and calculated live inverter/conversion loss power. Disabled by default because these are diagnostic intermediate values.
data_description.create_smart_meter_derived_sensors: Creates additional netted sensors, gross phase-flow sensors, and calculated home consumption from Jackery reported live home load when available, otherwise Smart Meter phase values plus Jackery grid-side input/output. Disable if you only want raw Jackery values.
data_description.enable_ble_transport: Starts the experimental local BLE listener for decoded/raw SolarVault BLE diagnostics. Requires Bluetooth access on the Home Assistant host and is disabled by default.
data_description.enable_ble_writes: Allows experimental local BLE command writes when the BLE transport is connected. Disabled by default; use only while testing direct SolarVault control.
data_description.enable_derived_home_energy_fallback: If sys/home/trends is unavailable, derives home_energy from device_home_stat.totalOutGridEnergy. Derived values are marked in attributes and source diagnostics.
data_description.enable_month_statistics: Imports the month period payload into the matching sensor.* and jackery_solarvault:*_month_daily statistic series. The hour-by-hour day-hourly external series stays imported regardless.
data_description.enable_unredacted_diagnostics: Disables redaction in the diagnostics export and payload debug JSONL for local troubleshooting. It exposes credentials, serial numbers, device IDs, MQTT topics and bluetoothKey values. Turn it off before sharing files.
data_description.enable_week_statistics: Imports the week period payload into the matching sensor.* and jackery_solarvault:*_week_daily statistic series. The hour-by-hour day-hourly external series stays imported regardless.
data_description.enable_year_statistics: Imports the year period payload into the matching sensor.* and jackery_solarvault:*_year_monthly statistic series. The hour-by-hour day-hourly external series stays imported regardless.
data_description.local_mqtt_enable: Subscribes to the SolarVault's local MQTT broker for LAN telemetry. Cloud MQTT pauses while the local connection is active; the cloud HTTP API keeps polling.
data_description.local_mqtt_host: IP or hostname of the local MQTT broker the SolarVault publishes to, for example 192.168.x.x.
data_description.third_party_mqtt_enable: Asks the SolarVault to publish telemetry to your own MQTT broker (PROTOCOL.md §5 / §15). Experimental: the cloud REST relay for this command is server-side blocked, so the integration publishes the SET_THIRD_PARTY_MQTT_CONFIG (actionId 3046) frame directly via BLE first and then cloud MQTT. Username, password, and token are encoded with the app-compatible ThirdPartMQTTConfig codec before sending.
data_description.third_party_mqtt_ip: IP or hostname of your local MQTT broker, for example 192.168.x.x.
data_description.third_party_mqtt_port: TCP port of your MQTT broker. Default is 1883.
data_description.third_party_mqtt_token: MQTT bridge token. If left blank, the integration auto-generates a stable 9-digit app-style fallback token.
data_description.third_party_mqtt_topic_filter: MQTT topic filter for the direct local broker fallback. Leave empty unless you know the exact Jackery bridge topic. `homeassistant` is usually wrong because it only matches Home Assistant's own event stream.
data_description.third_party_mqtt_username: Optional MQTT broker username. Leave blank for anonymous brokers.

reconfigure_credentials.description: Update the password, optional sensor toggles, and the experimental Third-Party MQTT bridge for the Jackery account {username}. The email must match the originally configured account so the entry's unique ID stays stable. Switch accounts by removing the entry first.
reconfigure_credentials.title: Reconfigure Jackery SolarVault

shelly.description: Link Shelly cloud (Cloud-to-Cloud) accessories to the Jackery account {username}. A browser opens so you can authorize Shelly; the Jackery cloud completes the account link. Return to Home Assistant once you have authorized — the integration then confirms the bound devices and reloads so their entities appear.
shelly.title: Pair Shelly cloud accessories

data_description.create_calculated_power_sensors: Creates optional local net-power sensors from values already delivered by Jackery: main battery charge minus discharge, battery-system/stack charge minus discharge, and grid-side input minus output. These sensors are useful for dashboards that need a signed net value, but they are not separate app values. Disabled by default so raw Jackery values stay unambiguous.
data_description.create_savings_detail_sensors: Creates optional sensors for the savings calculation: calculated savings, self-consumed AC energy, price, battery system loss energy, inverter/conversion loss energy, PV residual after self-consumption and calculated live inverter/conversion loss power. Disabled by default because these are diagnostic intermediate values.
data_description.create_smart_meter_derived_sensors: Creates additional netted sensors, gross phase-flow sensors, and calculated home consumption from Jackery reported live home load when available, otherwise Smart Meter phase values plus Jackery grid-side input/output. Disable if you only want raw Jackery values.
data_description.enable_ble_transport: Starts the experimental local BLE listener for decoded/raw SolarVault BLE diagnostics. Requires Bluetooth access on the Home Assistant host and is disabled by default.

user.description:
Enter your Jackery cloud credentials. Device, region and MQTT credentials are discovered automatically from Jackery's cloud/app protocol; manual IDs are intentionally not exposed.

The optional calculated net-power sensors are local derivations from Jackery raw values. Enable them only if you need an additional signed net value for dashboards.

Jackery usually allows only one active session per account. Keep the Jackery app signed out of the SolarVault account while Home Assistant should receive live MQTT updates. If the app is opened briefly, Home Assistant pauses MQTT reconnects for a short time, keeps polling over HTTP, and resumes live updates automatically.
user.title: Jackery SolarVault

savings_pv_residual_year_energy.name: PV residual after self-consumption yearly energy

confirm.description:
Jackery returned contradictory app/cloud statistics. {count} warning(s) were detected. First affected metric: {metric}. Examples: {examples}. Entity states are not silently repaired; enable logger custom_components.jackery_solarvault.payload_debug=debug, then use payload debug log jackery_solarvault_payload_debug.jsonl and diagnostics to compare raw payload values, parsed chart series and documented source sections in docs/PROTOCOL.md (§2 HTTP endpoints, §8 data-source priority).

Confirm to force a refresh from Jackery's cloud. Transient inconsistencies clear once a fresh consistent payload arrives. If the warning returns, file an issue at the integration tracker with the diagnostic dump.
confirm.title: Force a Jackery cloud refresh

confirm.description:
The Jackery device {device_id} reports activated=0 in the cloud system. This is a cloud-side data-quality flag, not proof that the device is unpaired locally. Some Jackery trend, statistic, and history endpoints may return empty or zero data while the cloud reports this flag.

To check this:
1. Open the Jackery mobile app
2. Confirm the device is registered and shows as 'Online'
3. Return here and force a cloud refresh

The repair issue will be dismissed once Jackery cloud reports activated=1, or when the cloud stops returning the inconsistent flag.
confirm.title: Force a Jackery cloud refresh

device_not_activated.title: Jackery device not activated in cloud

init.description: Calculated sensors are optional local derivations. The Smart Meter sensors calculate netted values, gross phase-flow values, and home consumption from Jackery reported live home load when available, otherwise Smart Meter net power plus Jackery grid-side input/output; they are not additional Jackery app raw values. The Third-Party MQTT bridge is experimental and asks the SolarVault to publish telemetry to your local broker as documented in PROTOCOL.md §5.
init.title: Jackery options

bind_smart_part.description: Bind a smart accessory (for example a Smart Plug) to the selected device over MQTT.

accessory_sn.description: Serial number of the smart accessory to bind, printed on the accessory or visible in the Jackery app.
accessory_sn.name: Accessory serial number

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

bind_smart_part.name: Bind smart part

delete_electricity_strategy.description: Delete an electricity strategy plan from the device.

body.description: Strategy body as JSON, usually just needs the tid (task ID).
body.name: Body

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device.
device_id.name: Device ID

delete_electricity_strategy.name: Delete electricity strategy

delete_storm_alert.description: Delete a specific weather or storm alert by alert ID over MQTT.

alert_id.description: Alert identifier from the weather plan diagnostic attributes, usually storm[].alertId.
alert_id.name: Alert ID

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly (visible in diagnostics under raw_api.system_list_response.data[].devices[].deviceId).
device_id.name: Device ID

delete_storm_alert.name: Delete storm alert

get_share_qr_code.description: Fetch the share QR code for the Jackery account that owns the selected device. Returns the response only.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. The QR code is resolved on the owning account; automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

get_share_qr_code.name: Get share QR code

insert_electricity_strategy.description: Add a new electricity strategy plan (charge/discharge) to the device.

body.description: Strategy body as JSON. Fields documented in source-of-truth.
body.name: Body

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device.
device_id.name: Device ID

insert_electricity_strategy.name: Add electricity strategy

list_shared_devices.description: List the devices shared with the Jackery account that owns the selected device. Returns the response only.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. The shared-device list is resolved on the owning account; automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

list_shared_devices.name: List shared devices

list_shared_managers.description: List the managers of a shared-device binding on the owning Jackery account. Returns the response only.

bind_user_id.description: User ID that owns the shared binding.
bind_user_id.name: Bind user ID

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. The managers are resolved on the owning account; automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

level.description: Share level filter. Only managers at this level are returned. Defaults to 0.
level.name: Share level

list_shared_managers.name: List shared managers

query_electricity_strategy.description: Query all electricity strategy plans from the device.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device.
device_id.name: Device ID

query_electricity_strategy.name: Query electricity strategy

query_third_party_mqtt_config.description: Query the experimental third-party MQTT bridge config over the existing Jackery MQTT command channel.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

query_third_party_mqtt_config.name: Query third-party MQTT config

refresh_subdevices.description: Force a rediscovery of accessories and sub-devices from the Jackery cloud, then refresh all entities. Use this after physically adding or removing an accessory.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly (visible in diagnostics under raw_api.system_list_response.data[].devices[].deviceId).
device_id.name: Device ID

refresh_subdevices.name: Refresh sub-devices

refresh_weather_plan.description: Query the current weather and storm plan from the device. The response is exposed in the Weather plan diagnostic sensor attributes.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly (visible in diagnostics under raw_api.system_list_response.data[].devices[].deviceId).
device_id.name: Device ID

refresh_weather_plan.name: Refresh weather and storm plan

remove_all_shared_access.description: Remove every shared access for a user at a share level on the owning Jackery account, then refresh.

bind_user_id.description: User ID whose shared access entries are removed.
bind_user_id.name: Bind user ID

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. The removal is performed on the owning account; automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

level.description: Share level to remove. Defaults to 0.
level.name: Share level

remove_all_shared_access.name: Remove all shared access

remove_shared_access.description: Remove a single shared access for a user on the owning Jackery account, then refresh.

bind_user_id.description: User ID whose shared access is removed.
bind_user_id.name: Bind user ID

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. The removal targets this device on the owning account; automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

remove_shared_access.name: Remove shared access

rename_system.description: Rename a Jackery SolarVault system through the Jackery cloud API. The name is shown in the Jackery app and Home Assistant device info.

system_id.description: Numeric Jackery system ID. It is visible in diagnostics under raw_api.system_list_response.data[].id.
system_id.name: System ID

rename_system.name: Rename SolarVault system

send_ble_command.description: Send one experimental binary BLE command frame to the selected device. Requires BLE transport, BLE writes and an active BLE connection.

ack_timeout.description: How long to wait, in seconds, for the device to echo a notify frame after the write. Ignored when Wait for ACK is off.
ack_timeout.name: ACK timeout

body.description: JSON object body to encrypt and write. Include the body-level cmd field when the target firmware command expects it.
body.name: Body

cmd.description: Binary BLE header command ID, 1 to 65535.
cmd.name: Command

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

flags.description: Binary BLE header flags, usually 0 for outbound test commands.
flags.name: Flags

wait_for_ack.description: Wait for the device to echo a decoded notify frame before considering the write successful. Recommended for setters so a silent firmware drop fails the call instead of being lost.
wait_for_ack.name: Wait for ACK

send_ble_command.name: Send BLE command

send_device_schedule.description: Publish a DownloadDeviceSchedule (cmd=112) frame to the selected device. Pick the actionId for ADD (3015), DELETE (3016), UPDATE (3017) or READ (3018) and pass the schedule body verbatim. Body shape is from the Frida-PCAP capture in docs/Markdown/MQTT_PROTOCOL.md.

body.description: Schedule body as JSON. Typical fields: actionType, taskType, mode, pw (W), sysSwitch (0/1), start/end (HH:MM), loops (7-char Mon-Sun bitmask), tid (task ID). The integration adds cmd=112 automatically.
body.name: Body

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

send_device_schedule.name: Send device schedule

set_device_nickname.description: Set a custom nickname for a Jackery device through the Jackery cloud API. The nickname is shown in the Jackery app and Home Assistant device info.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

set_device_nickname.name: Set device nickname

set_third_party_mqtt_config.description: Set the experimental third-party MQTT bridge config over the existing Jackery MQTT command channel.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

set_third_party_mqtt_config.name: Set third-party MQTT config

unbind_device.description: Unbind (remove) a device from the Jackery account through the Jackery cloud API. This is destructive: the device and its entities are removed from Home Assistant after the next refresh.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

unbind_device.name: Unbind device

unbind_smart_part.description: Unbind (remove) a smart accessory from the selected device over MQTT.

accessory_sn.description: Serial number of the smart accessory to unbind, printed on the accessory or visible in the Jackery app.
accessory_sn.name: Accessory serial number

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device. Automations may also pass the numeric Jackery device ID directly.
device_id.name: Device ID

unbind_smart_part.name: Unbind smart part

update_electricity_strategy.description: Update an existing electricity strategy plan on the device.

device_id.description: Pick a Jackery device.
device_id.name: Device ID
